request.question: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of adding trehalose to a freezing medium is covered in paper [4], which discusses trehalose in the context of cryopreservation, although it focuses on cell-laden hydrogel microspheres rather than HeLa cells specifically. The biological system of HeLa cells is not directly addressed in the retrieved papers, but the general context of cryopreservation and post-thaw viability is present in multiple studies. The experiment type of comparative cryopreservation is also reflected in the literature, particularly in papers discussing the effects of different cryoprotectants on viability outcomes.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved in a freezing medium supplemented with 250 mM trehalose versus DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be divided into two groups: one group will be cryopreserved using a freezing medium containing 250 mM trehalose, and the other group will use DMSO as a cryoprotectant. After thawing, cells will be allowed to recover for 30 minutes at 37°C before assessing viability using trypan blue exclusion. Each condition will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved in 10% DMSO freezing medium.
HeLa cells without any cryoprotectant in the freezing medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 250 mL of freezing medium by mixing 10% (v/v) DMSO in RPMI 1640 medium (Gibco, Cat# 11875-093) and 250 mM trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, Cat# T9531) in sterile water. Filter sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter (Millipore, Cat# SLGP033RB) and store at -20°C until use.
Equipment: Pipettes, Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes, 0.22 µm filter, Refrigerator
Reagents: 10% DMSO in RPMI 1640, 250 mM trehalose in sterile water
Expected outcome: Freezing medium is prepared and sterile, ready for use in cryopreservation.
Safety: DMSO is a hazardous material; handle with gloves and goggles.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in T-75 flasks (Corning, Cat# 353136) in RPMI 1640 medium supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Gibco, Cat# 26140-079) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin (Gibco, Cat# 15140-122) at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator until 80% confluence is reached (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: T-75 flasks, 5% CO2 incubator
Reagents: RPMI 1640 medium, 10% FBS, 1% penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluence, ready for harvesting.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, Cat# 25200-056) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize with complete RPMI 1640 medium and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL of the prepared freezing medium (either trehalose or DMSO).
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5810R), Pipettes, Tissue culture hood
Reagents: 0.25% trypsin-EDTA, Complete RPMI 1640 medium
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully detached, counted, and resuspended in freezing medium.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1 x 10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials (Nunc, Cat# 374202). Place cryovials in a controlled-rate freezer (Planer Kryo 10 Series) and cool at -1°C/min to -80°C. Transfer cryovials to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage.
Equipment: Controlled-rate freezer (Planer Kryo 10 Series), Cryovials, Liquid nitrogen storage tank
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully frozen and stored in liquid nitrogen.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Thawing Cells
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately thaw in a 37°C water bath for approximately 2-3 min until just thawed. Transfer the contents to a 15 mL conical tube and dilute with 10 mL of complete RPMI 1640 medium. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature and discard supernatant.
Equipment: 37°C water bath, Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5810R), 15 mL conical tubes
Reagents: Complete RPMI 1640 medium
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully thawed and diluted in complete medium.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Post-Thaw Recovery
Resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of complete RPMI 1640 medium and incubate at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery before viability assessment.
Equipment: 37°C incubator
Reagents: Complete RPMI 1640 medium
Expected outcome: Cells recover for 30 min at 37°C, ready for viability assessment.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Mix 10 µL of the cell suspension with 10 µL of trypan blue (Gibco, Cat# 15250-061) and load onto a hemocytometer. Count viable (unstained) and non-viable (stained) cells under a microscope (Olympus CKX53) at 10x magnification.
Equipment: Hemocytometer, Microscope (Olympus CKX53)
Reagents: Trypan blue solution
Expected outcome: Viability percentage calculated from cell counts, with a threshold of >70% viability for successful cryopreservation.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate the percentage of viable cells for each condition (trehalose and DMSO) using the formula: (number of viable cells / total cells) × 100. Perform statistical analysis using a two-tailed t-test to compare the means of the two groups (n=3).
Equipment: Computer with statistical software (e.g., GraphPad Prism)
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined between the two cryopreservation methods, with p < 0.05 considered significant.

DAY 2 | 2 days | Replication
Repeat the entire experiment (steps 1-8) two additional times to ensure reproducibility of results and validate findings across multiple trials.
Equipment: All previously listed equipment
Expected outcome: Consistent results across three independent replicates, confirming the reliability of the findings.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Trehalose enhances post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to DMSO.
The freezing and thawing protocols are optimized for HeLa cells.
Cell viability can be accurately assessed using trypan blue exclusion.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability in trehalose group is ≥ 70% and significantly higher than DMSO group (p < 0.05).
If viability in both groups is < 70%, reassess freezing conditions or concentrations used.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of HeLa cells for all experiments to minimize variability.
Standardize the freezing and thawing rates in the controlled-rate freezer.
Ensure that all reagents are from the same lot number to maintain consistency.

2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.
